title: Serve exposes doctor and providers metadata endpoints
description: Black-box smoke for `armature serve` that fetches both `/doctor` and `/providers` from a temporary isolated runtime home.
input: Start `armature serve`, fetch `/doctor` and `/providers`, and verify the server still exposes the same project/provider metadata as the CLI diagnostics surfaces.
command: bash -lc 'REPO_ROOT=${PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}; cd "$REPO_ROOT"; bash agent-eval/scripts/prepare-fast-gate.sh >/dev/null; TMP_HOME=$(mktemp -d); PORT=$(node -e '"'"'const net=require("node:net"); const s=net.createServer(); s.listen(0,"127.0.0.1",()=>{console.log(s.address().port); s.close();});'"'"'); env -i PATH="$PATH" HOME="$TMP_HOME" ARMATURE_HOME="$TMP_HOME/.armature" ARMATURE_PROVIDER=openai OPENAI_API_KEY=test-openai-key node dist/bin/armature.js serve --port "$PORT" > "$TMP_HOME/serve.out" 2>&1 & SERVER_PID=$!; trap "kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null" EXIT; bash agent-eval/scripts/wait-for-http.sh "http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/providers" "^(200)$" 50 0.1; echo "--- providers ---"; curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/providers"; echo; echo "--- doctor ---"; curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/doctor"'
